description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the ruby:2.3.1 Docker image with a postgres:latest service, caches the vendor/ruby directory, and defines DATABASE_URL, POSTGRES_DB, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, POSTGRES_USER variables. Add a test job in the test stage that runs apt-get install -y lcov then npm run coverage:test after executing the global before_script (ruby -v; apt-get update -qq; apt-get install curl; curl -sL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_7.x | bash -; apt-get install -qq nodejs; npm set progress=false; npm install --silent; gem install bundler --no-ri --no-rdoc; bundle install -j $(nproc) --path vendor; rails db:migrate; rails db:seed). Add three deploy jobs (deploy_to_sit, deploy_to_staging, production) in the deploy stage that each run the same before_script, then install dpl, deploy to the respective Heroku app with dpl --provider=heroku --app=... --api-key=$HEROKU_API_KEY, and trigger a Rails DB migration via a curl POST to the Heroku API. The sit and staging jobs run only on the develop branch and target the development environment, while the production job runs only on tags and targets the production environment.
